sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 65.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
sections.subtitleText: 65.2g of fat. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 13.7g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
sections.subtitleText: 13.7g of carbs. starch is doing most of the heavy lifting here. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 15.2g of protein. essential amino acid support is one of the better parts here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
sections.subtitleText: 15.2g of protein. essential amino acid support is one of the better parts here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 38% daily value, zinc at 21% daily value, iron at 16% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 38% daily value, zinc at 21% daily value, iron at 16% daily value stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side: contains useful polyphenols. adds plant-compound value beyond the core table. has strong healthy-fat branding appeal. a practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats. can be very satiating. dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

displayItems.title: contains useful polyphenols
displayItems.explanation: Adds extra plant-compound value beyond the core table.

displayItems.title: has strong healthy-fat branding appeal
displayItems.explanation: A practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats.

displayItems.title: can be very satiating
displayItems.explanation: Dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

sections.subtitleText: on the plus side: contains useful polyphenols. adds plant-compound value beyond the core table. has strong healthy-fat branding appeal. a practical content advantage because viewers already associate walnuts with healthy fats. can be very satiating. dense fats and texture can help fullness in practice.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next: easy to overeat calories quickly. energy density adds up fast. contains some anti-nutrients. phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can go rancid if stored badly. a practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

displayItems.title: contains some anti-nutrients
displayItems.explanation: Phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat.

displayItems.title: can go rancid if stored badly
displayItems.explanation: A practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

sections.subtitleText: the drawbacks next: easy to overeat calories quickly. energy density adds up fast. contains some anti-nutrients. phytates may reduce mineral uptake somewhat. can go rancid if stored badly. a practical downside versus some sturdier staples.

closing.summary: Walnuts has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Fats are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in A tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in A tier with an overall score of 57 from a base nutrition score of 57. Context barely changed the result.
